bnb-safari.label: CASE STUDY | 2025
bnb-safari.title: BNB SAFARI
bnb-safari.description: A self-initiated booking platform built to solve a real problem in the Kenyan hospitality space — replacing informal, conversation-heavy reservation flows with a clean, direct booking experience tailored for local operators.

challenge.heading: THE CHALLENGE
challenge.text: Most small BnB operators in Kenya were running bookings through WhatsApp threads — no availability calendar, no payment confirmation, no paper trail. Every inquiry meant a back-and-forth conversation before anything was confirmed.
challenge.subtext: The goal was to build a lightweight booking engine that gave property owners real-time control over their listings and gave guests a faster, more trustworthy path to checkout — without the overhead of a full OTA platform.

strategy.title: LISTING MANAGEMENT
strategy.description: Built an operator dashboard that lets BnB hosts manage availability, pricing, and property details in real time — no technical knowledge required.

strategy.title: GUEST EXPERIENCE
strategy.description: Designed a browsing and booking flow optimized for mobile — full-bleed photography, availability calendar, and property details surfaced without friction.

strategy.title: LOCAL PAYMENTS
strategy.description: Integrated M-Pesa via the Safaricom Daraja API to handle reservations and deposits natively — no card required, no foreign payment friction.

results.label: To Confirm a Booking
results.description: End-to-end reservation time from browsing to M-Pesa payment confirmation.

results.label: Mobile-First
results.description: Designed and tested exclusively for the devices Kenyan guests and operators actually use.

results.label: WhatsApp Steps Required
results.description: Guests complete the full booking flow without leaving the platform.

freelanceboard.label: CASE STUDY | 2026
freelanceboard.title: FREELANCEBOARD
freelanceboard.description: A personal operations system built to run my own freelance practice — replacing five separate tools with one dashboard that handles time tracking, client management, invoicing, and AI-assisted lead generation.

challenge.heading: THE CHALLENGE
challenge.text: Running a freelance practice means context-switching between invoicing tools, project trackers, communication threads, and spreadsheets — none of which talk to each other.
challenge.subtext: Rather than duct-taping third-party tools together, I built one system specifically around how I actually work — with the flexibility to add modules as my workflow evolves.

strategy.title: MODULAR DASHBOARD
strategy.description: Built around discrete, independently functional modules — time tracking, invoicing, client records, project milestones — so the system grows without becoming brittle.

strategy.title: PAYMENT PROTECTION
strategy.description: Implemented a three-gate payment flow that ties milestone completion to invoice generation, reducing the risk of unpaid work slipping through.

strategy.title: AI LEAD GENERATION
strategy.description: Built a leads module that scrapes Google Maps for potential clients and uses an AI layer to auto-generate tailored service proposals — turning cold outreach into a semi-automated pipeline.

results.label: Modules Scoped
results.description: Covering the full freelance workflow from lead capture to invoice collection.

results.label: Tool Instead of Five
results.description: Consolidated time tracking, invoicing, project management, and client comms into a single system.

results.label: Lead Pipeline
results.description: Automated proposal generation from Maps scraping — cuts cold outreach time significantly.

damtite-ems.label: CASE STUDY | 2025
damtite-ems.title: DAMTITE EMS
damtite-ems.description: A workforce operations platform built to centralize employee management, project tracking, and automated payroll for an engineering and construction firm — replacing fragmented paper and spreadsheet systems with a single mobile-optimized source of truth.

challenge.heading: THE FRAGMENTATION BARRIER
challenge.text: Field operations at engineering firms run on disconnected systems — attendance tracked on paper, tools logged in spreadsheets, payroll calculated manually in isolation. The gaps between these create compounding errors and no single view of what's actually happening on site.
challenge.subtext: The objective was a mobile-optimized platform that bridges site workers and office management — giving both sides real-time operational visibility without adding workflow friction.

strategy.title: ROLE-BASED ACCESS
strategy.description: Built a three-tier permission system — Admin, Manager, Worker — with granular access controls tailored to how each role actually operates, whether in the office or on site.

strategy.title: AUTOMATED PAYROLL
strategy.description: Engineered a payroll calculation engine that pulls directly from attendance logs, approved leave records, and advance requests — removing manual computation from the process entirely.

strategy.title: FIELD-TO-OFFICE SYNC
strategy.description: Developed real-time tool request workflows and image-based proof-of-work uploads — giving office management verifiable daily progress reports straight from the site.

results.label: Access Control
results.description: Distinct permission layers for Workers, Managers, and Admins — each scoped to their operational context.

results.label: Integrated Modules
results.description: Payroll, attendance, leave, tool requests, project tracking, and more — consolidated into one system.

results.label: Field Optimized
results.description: Built for reliability on-site — workers submit attendance, tool requests, and proof-of-work from their phones.
